books.title: Living with What You've Lost
books.subtitle: A Nervous System Reset
books.desc: Something in you is gone, and no one named it a loss. keep caring without losing myself.
books.long_desc:
Something in you is gone, and no one named it a loss.

This book is for mid-level manager running a cross-functional teams who I care too much and it's breaking me. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the quiet grief of a self that's been spent, and how absence keeps making itself present. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.

Book 3 of the Somatic Wisdom compassion fatigue series.

You're sure that if they really saw you, it would be over.

This book is for senior engineer / PM at public tech companys who I always end up back at 'I'm not enough'. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the exposure-fear that keeps you bracing to be found out, and why it runs so deep. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.

Book 1 of the Somatic Wisdom self worth series.

However well you do, someone is always further ahead.

This book is for working parent of young kidss who I always end up back at 'I'm not enough'. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the reflex that measures your worth against others, and what it costs to keep score. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.
